title: hoya parasitica var. citrina
intro: Hoya parasitica var. citrina is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the citrine waxy, which is a variety of the Hoya parasitica plant. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Hoya parasitica var. citrina include its potential to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide a range of medicinal benefits and support overall well-being.
